texts.full_text: at Reopening Old Wounds [SPOILERS] By Adam Cohen | May 20, 2013 - 10:04 pm [WARNING! SPOILERS AHEAD] About three weeks before STAR TrEK INTO DARKNESS (STID) was released in the U.S., I shamelessly tracked down posts on Trek message boards for any single spoiler review following the initial press screenings. I wanted to know the truth behind everything: John Harrison, whether the Enterprise is destroyed and what about those pesky Klingons? I got a lot more than I expected from a couple of reviews that gave excruciatingly detailed scene-by-scene breakdowns of the entire film. As I scanned the posts, my eyes began to widen at the most unbelievable (and at the time unforgiveable) revelations: Kirk dies saving the enterprise, the death scene from THE WRATH OF KHANis replicated almost beat for beat with Harrison replacing Khan as the villain and saying all

texts.full_text: nd is the world average [@PDG]. The boundaries of the thick part of the error bars represent the values of $\alpha_s(M_Z)$ for which the $\chi^2-\chi^2_{min}=1$. These error estimates do not include scale variation. The thin error bars show the uncertainty including the scale uncertainties.[]{data-label="fig:all"}](all_cubicFit_LHC.png "fig:") To estimate the share of the uncertainties due to each error source we use the quantities $$\label{eq:share} \chi_s^2=\left(y_{t}(\alpha_s(M_Z))-y_{d}\right)^TC_{tot}^{-1}C_sC_{tot}^{-1}\left(y_{t}(\alpha_s(M_Z))-y_{d}\right)$$ defined for each error source covariance matrix $C_s$. The $\chi_s^2$ sum up to the total $\chi^2$. We assign each error source a fraction $\chi^2_s/\chi^2$ of the total uncertainty. In the limit where all errors are fully uncorrelated this procedure is equivalent to summing the errors in quadrature. Figure \[fig:share\] shows an example of how the uncertainty is shared between the error sources for CT14. The figure shows the share for each individual distribution, for the combination of all transverse momentum distributions and rapidities, for the multiplicity combination and for the full combination. ![ Share of the uncertainty by error source according to Eq. \[eq:share\]. The results are for the PDF set CT14.[]{data-label="fig:share"}](share_CT14.png) We can see that the dominant share of the uncertainty comes from the experimental uncertainties. In principle the uncertainty could be reduced by increasing the statistical accuracy of the theory prediction and the understanding of the non-perturbative corrections. Table \[tab:result\] shows the result for the best fit ${\alpha_S(M_Z)}$ for a list of PDF sets. The uncertainties in this table do not include scale variation and non-perturbative correction uncertainties. The theory and experimental uncertainties are approximately of the same size while the PDF uncertainty is almost one order of magnitude smaller. The $\chi^2$ per degree of freedom in slightly below 1 and is very similar across PDF sets. [ccccc]{} Table \[tab:result\_scale\] shows scale variation uncertainty estimates using the two methods outlined in section \[sec:scale\]. The second and third columns vary the factorisation and renormalisation scales by factors of $1/2$, $1$ or $2$. The second column shows the resulting uncertainty if the same factor is chosen for both scales, and the

texts.full_text: rich history, cultural landmarks, and vibrant local life. The key contributors to these impressive numbers are tourists from places like the United Kingdom who have consistently chosen Spain as their top travel destination within Europe, along with significant surges coming from other regions such as Russia where tourism figures doubled last month. This notable progress is a result of both an increasing awareness and interest in Spanish culture worldwide, as well as enhanced global accessibility facilitated through improved transportation links. The increased tourist inflow not only benefits Spain's economy by bolstering sectors like hospitality but also plays a pivotal role in fostering international cultural exchange and understanding. The continued growth in tourism numbers from such varied regions highlights the success of Spain's efforts to attract diverse populations, emphasizing its position as an attractive destination for tourists worldwide. As Spain continues to welcome more visitors from around the globe, it is essential that the country maintains a focus on sustainability within its tourism sector, ensuring that it can continue to provide these unique experiences without compromising its rich culture and heritage for future generations. This balance between economic benefit and conservation efforts will be key in supporting long-term growth while safeguarding Spain's diverse historical legacy against potential threats posed by over-tourism. In conclusion, the significant rise in international tourism to Spain, reflected in the latest statistics on visitor numbers from various regions worldwide like the UK, Russia, South America, and Portugal, signifies a promising year for its travel industry. Such success not only points towards an increased global interest in exploring everything that Spain has to offer but also serves as a testament to the country's continued efforts in nurturing inter
